title: Figure 1 in Assessing grass carp (Ctenopharyngodon idella) occupancy and detection probability within Lake Erie from environmental DNA
author: Bopp, Justin
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1. Map denoting all monthly grass carp eDNA sampling events in 2018 (A–C) and 2019 (D–F) aggregated at each sampling location (Hot Ponds, Detroit River, and North Maumee Bay) and acoustic receiver locations (grey circles) in the western basin of Lake Erie. Positive and negative eDNA detections, defined as at least one positive qPCR detection on one replicate among all markers (GCTM10, GCTM22, GCTM32) are denoted by orange crosses and pink triangles, respectively. The 3 grass carp captured from conventional gear (total sampling events = 451) in the Detroit River (October 2018), Hot Pond (July 2019) and North Maumee Bay (July 2019) are denoted by a yellow hexagon.

The 3 grass carp captured from conventional gear (total sampling events = 451) in the Detroit River (October 2018), Hot Pond (July 2019) and North Maumee Bay (July 2019) are denoted by a yellow hexagon.
